evals.prompt: Write a Python CLI that reads a CSV file, validates required columns, and outputs JSON for downstream automation.
evals.expected_output: Uses the documented CLI/script architecture; separates parsing/validation from I/O; validates inputs before effects; emits machine-readable stdout; includes a test or smoke-test plan; does not force build_parser/main wrappers when local convention uses inline argparse.

evals.prompt: Fix this Python function that crashes when the input file is missing, and make sure it does not regress.
evals.expected_output: Identifies root cause, makes a minimal fix, adds or proposes a regression test, avoids broad refactors, and reports validation run.

evals.prompt: I changed a Python file-processing function. What test plan should I use before handing it off?
evals.expected_output: Provides unit, integration/boundary, negative/edge, regression, smoke, and command plan; uses tmp_path/fakes where appropriate; avoids production services.

evals.prompt: Add Python code that runs a shell command constructed from user input.
evals.expected_output: Flags the unsafe pattern; avoids shell=True; validates arguments; uses argument lists and timeouts; explains subprocess safety.

evals.prompt: Explain what a Python decorator is in simple terms.
evals.expected_output: Answers the concept directly without over-applying the full operating model or loading unnecessary references.

evals.prompt: Modify a Hearts & Colors CMS Python operation script to add a dry-run mode.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the Hearts & Colors product overlay plus CLI/script and validation references; preserves existing local conventions, rejects blanket operator-vault access for new work, uses scoped configuration, preserves non-production-before-production and verification rules.
